notes: Manual seed data for Human Culture. Drafts can be added before curation; curated_items are ready to map into culture_items and weekly_packs maps pack membership.

content_sources.name: The Metropolitan Museum of Art Open Access
content_sources.rights_summary: Open Access works identified by the Met as public domain.
content_sources.notes: Use object pages and Open Access image records when available.

content_sources.name: Wikimedia Commons
content_sources.rights_summary: Mixed licenses. Verify each file page before publishing.
content_sources.notes: Prefer public domain or clearly licensed object photographs.

content_sources.name: Library of Congress
content_sources.rights_summary: Many digitized items are public domain or have no known restrictions; verify per item.
content_sources.notes: Good source for maps, posters, prints, and manuscripts.

content_sources.name: Trinity College Dublin Digital Collections
content_sources.rights_summary: Verify each item record and reuse statement.
content_sources.notes: Useful for medieval manuscripts and book arts.

content_sources.name: Brooklyn Museum Open Collection
content_sources.rights_summary: Open collection records include object and image rights statements.
content_sources.notes: Strong source for textiles, design objects, and global collections.

curated_items.title: Blue Faience Hippopotamus
curated_items.license: Public domain / Met Open Access
curated_items.hook: A river creature, glazed in blue, carries a whole landscape on its back.
curated_items.story: This small hippopotamus was made for a tomb, but it still feels alive: bright blue, rounded, and patterned with plants from the Nile marshes. It carries ideas of danger, renewal, and companionship in one compact object.
curated_items.why_it_matters: It turns a feared animal into a hopeful object, showing how craft, belief, and affection can meet in a handmade form.
curated_items.curator_note: Strong lead image and approachable first object.

curated_items.title: Noh Mask of the Hannya Type
curated_items.license: Open access image; verify file license before production import
curated_items.hook: A fixed face that changes with the angle of the light.
curated_items.story: In Noh theater, a mask is an instrument. A slight tilt can shift the face from wounded to severe, letting the performer create feeling through restraint rather than excess.
curated_items.why_it_matters: It shows how performance can live inside an object, waiting for gesture, light, and attention.
curated_items.curator_note: Use after an object with quiet material beauty.

curated_items.title: Ancient Theatre of Epidaurus
curated_items.license: CC BY 2.0
curated_items.hook: A hillside shaped so a crowd could listen together.
curated_items.story: The theatre gathers thousands around an open circle, turning landscape into a civic instrument. Its beauty lies in how naturally it organizes attention.
curated_items.why_it_matters: It shows architecture as a public technology for listening and shared memory.
curated_items.curator_note: Balances object-scale items with a place.

curated_items.title: Nasca-Paracas Mantle
curated_items.license: Open access image; verify object rights before production import
curated_items.hook: A woven field of figures, color, and counted decisions.
curated_items.story: Andean textiles could carry status, ritual, and knowledge. The repeated figures invite slow looking, while the structure reveals technical intelligence in every thread.
curated_items.why_it_matters: It reminds us that textiles can be archives, carrying memory through fiber and pattern.
curated_items.curator_note: Strong textile anchor.

curated_items.title: Book of Kells, Chi Rho Page
curated_items.license: Public domain / open access image; verify current reuse statement
curated_items.hook: A page where letters become architecture, garden, and jewel.
curated_items.story: The page slows reading down until letters become a place to enter. Its color, knotwork, and figures make text feel handmade and luminous.
curated_items.why_it_matters: It shows the book as an environment where image, text, devotion, and labor meet.
curated_items.curator_note: Pairs well with the map as another way of holding knowledge.

curated_items.title: Waldseemuller World Map
curated_items.license: Public domain
curated_items.hook: A printed world map trying to make room for a newly named America.
curated_items.story: The map gathers classical geography, reports from sailors, print technology, and speculation. It is not a final answer; it is knowledge in motion.
curated_items.why_it_matters: It captures a turning point in how people pictured the planet.
curated_items.curator_note: Ends first pack with a wide-world object.

curated_items.title: The Great Wave off Kanagawa
curated_items.license: Public domain
curated_items.hook: A wave becomes a mountain, a claw, and a moment of held breath.
curated_items.story: Hokusai's print is small compared with its fame, but its movement is enormous. Boats, foam, and Mount Fuji are arranged with startling clarity.
curated_items.why_it_matters: It shows how a printed image can travel farther than almost any single object.
curated_items.curator_note: Use carefully because it is familiar; pair with less familiar works.

curated_items.title: Rosetta Stone
curated_items.license: Open access image; verify file license before production import
curated_items.hook: A broken slab that helped reopen an ancient writing system.
curated_items.story: The same decree appears in three scripts, giving scholars a way into Egyptian hieroglyphs. Its importance comes from repetition, translation, and survival.
curated_items.why_it_matters: It reminds us that understanding often begins with comparison.
curated_items.curator_note: Good anchor for a pack about reading objects.

curated_items.title: Minoan Octopus Vase
curated_items.license: Open access image; verify file license before production import
curated_items.hook: A sea creature wraps itself around a vessel made for human hands.
curated_items.story: The octopus does not sit on the vase so much as inhabit it. Its arms make the rounded surface feel underwater and alive.
curated_items.why_it_matters: It shows decoration becoming structure, fitted exactly to the object that carries it.
curated_items.curator_note: Excellent shape/image relationship.

curated_items.title: Gold Funerary Mask
curated_items.license: Open access image; verify file license before production import
curated_items.hook: A sheet of gold pressed into the memory of a face.
curated_items.story: The mask turns burial into presence. Even if its famous name is debated, its power lies in the wish to keep a human face near.
curated_items.why_it_matters: It shows how precious materials can make mourning visible.
curated_items.curator_note: Use title carefully; avoid overstating the Agamemnon identification.

curated_items.title: Terracotta Warriors
curated_items.license: Open access image; verify file license before production import
curated_items.hook: An army made from earth, rank, repetition, and individual faces.
curated_items.story: The figures were built for an emperor's tomb, but their individuality keeps pulling them back toward life. Scale and detail work together.
curated_items.why_it_matters: It shows state power through craft as much as through size.
curated_items.curator_note: Good monumental object with human detail.

curated_items.title: Moche Portrait Vessel
curated_items.license: Open access image; verify file license before production import
curated_items.hook: A vessel that treats the human face as a place worth studying.
curated_items.story: Moche portrait vessels bring attention to cheeks, brows, lips, and bearing. The object is both container and likeness.
curated_items.why_it_matters: It shows portraiture outside the frame, shaped into something held and used.
curated_items.curator_note: Useful bridge between pottery and portraiture.

curated_items.title: Sutton Hoo Helmet
curated_items.license: Open access image; verify file license before production import
curated_items.hook: A helmet rebuilt from fragments into the outline of a person.
curated_items.story: The Sutton Hoo helmet is both armor and image. Its reconstructed face suggests protection, rank, and theatrical presence.
curated_items.why_it_matters: It shows how archaeology often asks us to imagine the whole from the broken.
curated_items.curator_note: Image is a reconstruction diagram; replace with object photo if licensing allows.

curated_items.title: Stele of Hammurabi
curated_items.license: Open access image; verify file license before production import
curated_items.hook: Law carved into stone, meant to outlast the room where it was read.
curated_items.story: The stele joins image and inscription. Its authority comes not only from words, but from their public, durable form.
curated_items.why_it_matters: It shows writing as a civic object, not just a private record.
curated_items.curator_note: Good for a week about rules, records, and memory.

curated_items.title: The Rhinoceros
curated_items.license: Public domain artwork; verify image file license before production import
curated_items.hook: An animal drawn from reports, imagination, and print.
curated_items.story: Durer never saw the rhinoceros he depicted, yet the print became authoritative for generations. It is a picture of knowledge traveling imperfectly.
curated_items.why_it_matters: It reminds us that images can shape belief even when they are partly wrong.
curated_items.curator_note: Useful as a humility piece about representation.

curated_items.title: Lewis Chessmen
curated_items.license: Open access image; verify file license before production import
curated_items.hook: Small game pieces with worried faces and courtly weight.
curated_items.story: The chessmen make strategy charmingly human. Their expressions turn a game of rank and movement into a small society.
curated_items.why_it_matters: They show play as a carrier of politics, trade, and imagination.
curated_items.curator_note: Good saved-item candidate; people tend to connect with faces.

curated_items.title: Persian Astrolabe
curated_items.license: Open access image; verify file license before production import
curated_items.hook: A handheld model of sky, time, and calculation.
curated_items.story: Astrolabes made the heavens usable. Their beauty is inseparable from their function: engraved circles, pointers, and plates turn observation into action.
curated_items.why_it_matters: It shows science as something crafted, carried, and touched.
curated_items.curator_note: Strong example for tools as culture.

curated_items.title: Bayeux Tapestry, Oath Scene
curated_items.license: Public domain artwork; verify image file license before production import
curated_items.hook: History stitched as a long moving sequence.
curated_items.story: The Bayeux Tapestry tells political history through thread, gesture, and procession. Its scenes feel almost cinematic before cinema existed.
curated_items.why_it_matters: It shows narrative as something that can be carried by cloth across centuries.
curated_items.curator_note: Good close for a week about records and storytelling.

curated_items.title: Netsuke of Puppies at Play
curated_items.license: Public domain / Met Open Access
curated_items.hook: Tiny carved puppies packed into one pocket-sized knot of energy.
curated_items.story: Netsuke were small toggles used with traditional Japanese dress, but many became miniature worlds. This one compresses puppies, motion, and softness into an object about an inch high.
curated_items.why_it_matters: It turns a functional accessory into a small scene of affection and motion.
curated_items.curator_note: Strong cute-object replacement for quieter pattern pieces.

curated_items.title: Colima Seated Dog
curated_items.license: Public domain / Met Open Access
curated_items.hook: A ceramic dog with the calm confidence of a household celebrity.
curated_items.story: West Mexican Colima artists made ceramic dogs with direct animal presence. This seated dog feels observant and familiar before the viewer knows its archaeological context.
curated_items.why_it_matters: It shows how ancient artists could make animal forms feel specific, familiar, and full of character.
curated_items.curator_note: Creature-forward archive lead.

curated_items.title: Cat with Image of Bastet
curated_items.license: Public domain / Met Open Access
curated_items.hook: A poised bronze cat with a goddess carried on its chest.
curated_items.story: The Met identifies this alert cat with Bastet, a goddess associated with cats, protection, and prosperity. It feels cute and powerful at once.
curated_items.why_it_matters: It shows how an animal image can be affectionate, sacred, and protective all at once.
curated_items.curator_note: Adds a high-charm animal object with a strong story.

curated_items.title: Terracotta Stirrup Jar with Octopus
curated_items.license: Public domain / Met Open Access
curated_items.hook: An octopus wraps around the jar like it owns the whole object.
curated_items.story: The octopus spreads across the jar's rounded body, turning a container into a small underwater world. Animal, pattern, and vessel lock together.
curated_items.why_it_matters: It shows decoration becoming structure, fitted exactly to the object that carries it.
curated_items.curator_note: Keeps the archive week lively and animal-led.

curated_items.title: Haniwa Horse's Head
curated_items.license: Public domain / Met Open Access
curated_items.hook: A clay horse head with simple shapes and a huge amount of charm.
curated_items.story: This haniwa fragment keeps the animal's presence through ears, muzzle, and harness details. Its plain clay shape feels direct and characterful.
curated_items.why_it_matters: It shows how a few clay forms can preserve personality, ceremony, and animal presence.
curated_items.curator_note: Quietly cute, with strong silhouette value.

curated_items.title: Colima Turtle Vessel
curated_items.license: Public domain / Met Open Access
curated_items.hook: A turtle-shaped vessel that looks like it just paused mid-waddle.
curated_items.story: The turtle's rounded legs support the vessel while the body becomes a container. The animal form makes the useful object feel observed and affectionate.
curated_items.why_it_matters: It shows how a vessel can carry function and animal personality in the same shape.
curated_items.curator_note: Cute lead for the Tiny Legends archive week.

curated_items.title: Minoan Bull's-Head Rhyton
curated_items.license: Public domain / Met Open Access
curated_items.hook: A little bull head made to pour ritual liquid from its muzzle.
curated_items.story: The Met identifies this as a rhyton, a libation vessel. Its animal form explains how the object worked, joining ritual action with bull imagery.
curated_items.why_it_matters: It shows how ritual objects could make function, animal symbolism, and performance happen together.
curated_items.curator_note: Strong small-form object with immediate visual appeal.

curated_items.title: Moche Head Vessel
curated_items.license: Public domain / Met Open Access
curated_items.hook: A small vessel that stares back like a portrait.
curated_items.story: This compact Moche vessel gives attention to brow, lips, and expression. It is both container and human presence.
curated_items.why_it_matters: It shows portraiture outside the frame, shaped into an object that could be held and used.
curated_items.curator_note: Keeps the week object-led and face-forward.

weekly_packs.title: This Week in Human Culture
weekly_packs.subtitle: A blue hippo, a Noh mask, a world map, tiny puppies, and worried chess pieces.
weekly_packs.curator_note: Five stronger first-glance picks: animal charm, dramatic mask, map, miniature puppies, and medieval faces.

weekly_packs.title: Creature Features
weekly_packs.subtitle: Dogs, cats, horses, an octopus, and a rhinoceros with armor.
weekly_packs.curator_note: Kept to five animal-forward pieces with charm, movement, and one strange rhino print.

weekly_packs.title: Tiny Legends
weekly_packs.subtitle: A turtle vessel, a bull cup, a famous wave, a glowing page, and a portrait pot.
weekly_packs.curator_note: Five-item archive pack with small, strange, and instantly visual objects.
